text: Artificial intelligence is changing our world rapidly, but it also raises important ethical questions. One major issue is bias in AI systems. If the data used to train AI is not diverse, the system may make unfair decisions, for example in hiring or loan applications. Fairness means ensuring AI treats all people equally, regardless of their background. Another concern is privacy, as AI often collects huge amounts of personal data, so people need to know how their information is used and protected. Transparency is also vital—users should understand how AI reaches its conclusions. Ultimately, responsible AI development requires clear rules and accountability to ensure technology benefits everyone safely and fairly.
